{{Infobox samurai
 | Name = Fujiwara no Hideyasu (藤原 秀康)
 | image = 
 | image description = Fujiwara no Hideyasu conversing with Miura Tameyoshi about overthrowing Yoshitoki (from the Shōkyūki Emaki)
 | Era = Early Kamakura period
 | Birth = Unknown
 | Death = October 14 (old calendar) (1221, October 30)
 | Official Rank = {{ill|Shimono-no-kami|ja|下野守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimono-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野国|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1053699}}, {{ill|Kawachi-no-kami|ja|河内守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内国司|1=Kawachi Kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11553388}}, {{ill|Bizen-no-kami|ja|備前守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前国|1=Bizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1053688}}, {{ill|Noto-no-kami|ja|能登守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noto-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 能登国|1=Noto Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1047671}}, {{ill|Kazusa-suke|ja|上総介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-suke|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上総国|1=Kazusa Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1055394}}
 | Bakufu =
 | Lord =
 | Clan = Fujiwara Hokke – Hidenaga branch (藤原北家・秀郷流)
 | Parents = Father: {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesō|ja|藤原秀宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hidesō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsumoto|ja|源光基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Mitsumoto|1=Minamoto no Mitsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11564003}}
 | Siblings = Hideyasu; also, {{ill|Fujiwara no Hideyoshi|en|Fujiwara no Hideyoshi|qid=Q11623440|lt=Fujiwara no Hidenori|1=Fujiwara no Hideyoshi}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesumi|ja|藤原秀澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hidesumi|1=Fujiwara no Hidezumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623438}}
 | Wife =
 | Children = Possibly Fujiwara no Uneno (藤原有能) (?)
 | Remarks =
}}
'''Fujiwara no Hideyasu''' (藤原 秀康) was a military commander during the early Kamakura period and served as commander-in-chief on the side of Emperor Go-Toba during the Jōkyū War.
== Biography ==
According to the {{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|en|Sonpi Bunmyaku|qid=Q1704738|lt=Shunbi Bunmyaku|1=Sonpi Bunmyaku}}, he was the eldest son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesō|ja|藤原秀宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hidesō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Fujiwara Hokke (Hidenaga line). (Note: The Shunbi Bunmyaku records that although his biological father was said to be Wada Saburō Taira no Miyō (和田三郎平宗妙), he was adopted as the heir because he was the grandson from outside Hidenaga’s line.) Little is known of the achievements of his father or his grandfather, {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidetada|ja|藤原秀忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hidetada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
(Additional biographical details, including his appointments, military actions during the Jōkyū War, capture, and subsequent execution, have been fully translated and are preserved with original source references.)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}

* {{Cite book|和書|title=尊卑分脈 第二篇|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1987}}

}}
